topic.title: Synchronizing automatically
topic.shortdesc: When enabled, automatic synchronization copies all (nonconflicting) remote changes to the local project and local changes to the remote system as needed. If conflicts are present, the Synchronize perspective opens so that you can resolve the conflicts. You can use two methods to enable automatic synchronization.

body.note: The remote synchronization function is deprecated.

section.title: Setting the automatic synchronization preference
section.p: You can set up a local project or file for automatic remote synchronization.
To enable automatic remote synchronization for a local project or file, right-click any remote-enabled file or project and then click RemoteAutomatically synchronize.

The Automatically synchronize action has a checkbox next to it when it is enabled. If automatic synchronization is not enabled and changes are found in the project, a warning is displayed. The warning indicates that project resources must be synchronized. Select the Always automatically synchronize checkbox to enable this action.

section.title: Remote synchronization during a syntax check
section.p: When a local project is enabled for remote synchronization, you can perform a remote syntax check.

A remote syntax check gathers the remote properties that you set when you enable remote synchronization, generates syntax check JCL, and submits the JCL to the remote system. Errors are reported in the Remote Error List.

To perform a remote syntax check, right-click the project or file, and click Syntax checkRemote
or press Ctrl+Alt+R.

If changes occurred to the file or other files in the project, a warning window prompts you to synchronize your changes.
To stop the syntax check and open the Synchronize perspective, click Yes. For more information, see .
To submit the syntax check JCL but not synchronize any changes, click No.
To stop the syntax check from being submitted, click Cancel.
You can avoid this warning by using the preference.
